items.title: Machlas benoemt in aanloop naar CL-duel Ajax hét probleem van de Eredivisie
items.summary: Nikos Machlas heeft vooruitgeblikt op de Champions League-wedstrijd tussen Ajax en Olympiakos Piraeus. De oud-speler van de Amsterdammers constateert dat Ajax tekenen van herstel vertoont, maar ziet ook kansen voor de Grieken. Daarnaast benoemde hij het probleem van de Eredivisie.

items.title: AS Monaco wil Noslin op huurbasis overnemen van Lazio
items.summary: Tijjani Noslin staat in de nadrukkelijke belangstelling van AS Monaco. De nummer tien van de Ligue 1 praat met Lazio over een tijdelijke overgang van de 26-jarige aanvaller.

items.title: Ex-FIFA prez backs call to 'stay away' from WC
items.summary: Former FIFA president Sepp Blatter on Monday backed a proposed fan boycott of World Cup matches in the United States because of the conduct of President Donald Trump and his administration at home and abroad.

items.title: Source: USMNT's Freeman set for Villarreal move
items.summary: LaLiga side Villarreal is finalizing the acquisition of United States and Orlando City defender Alex Freeman, a source with knowledge of the deal told ESPN.

items.title: PSG complete signing of Barça teen Fernández
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain have completed the signing of Barcelona's talented teenager Dro Fernández after exercising his €8 million ($9.5 million) release clause for the LaLiga side.

items.title: Sources: Man United fear Dorgu injury absence
items.summary: Manchester United fear Patrick Dorgu could be ruled out for a number of weeks, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: 'Geen extra motivatie nodig bij de spelers van PSV'
items.summary: PSV-trainer Peter Bosz weet dat het Champions League-duel met Bayern München alles in zich heeft om voor voldoende motivatie te zorgen bij zijn spelers.

items.title: Alcaraz's dominance sets up Australian Open semi-final date with Zverev
items.summary: Carlos Alcaraz ‌continued his bid for a first title at Melbourne Park and a career Grand Slam with a dominant 7-5 6-2 6-1 victory over home favourite Alex de Minaur.

items.title: KV Mechelen wint de strijd om Superboer Boersma
items.summary: Bouke Boersma vervolgt zijn carrière bij KV Mechelen. De twintigjarige spits van De Graafschap, die eveneens naar Go Ahead Eagles kon, zal spoedig een contract voor 3,5 jaar tekenen bij de Belgische club. KV Mechelen betaalt 1,1 miljoen euro voor de aanvaller. Dat bedrag kan met bonussen nog oplopen.

items.title: Bosz heeft nog niet volledige zekerheid over inzetbaarheid Til en Salah-Eddine
items.summary: PSV-trainer Peter Bosz gaat dinsdag beoordelen of Guus Til en Anass Salah-Eddine inzetbaar zijn tijdens de Champions League-wedstrijd tegen Bayern München. Het duo miste de wedstrijd tegen NAC Breda.

items.title: Man Utd's Dorgu set for weeks out with hamstring issue
items.summary: In-form Patrick Dorgu is still being assessed but he is likely to miss Manchester United matches with the injury he picked up against Arsenal.

items.title: Why are fitness trackers banned at Grand Slams and why are players unhappy?
items.summary: Carlos Alcaraz, Jannik Sinner and Aryna Sabalenka are among several ‌players banned from using wearable fitness trackers. But why are they banned at Grand Slams?

items.title: Luiz open to Villa return as Emery reignites interest
items.summary: Aston Villa make a fresh enquiry to re-sign former midfielder Douglas Luiz on loan as Unai Emery looks to bolster his options in the centre of the pitch.

items.title: As 'managers' disappear from the Premier League, are clubs really better for it?
items.summary: Premier League clubs are corporations that have outgrown the "manager" role that legendary Arsenal and Man United clubs once had. Why?

items.title: Removing US as World Cup host would be eminently sad – and entirely justified | Alexander Abnos
items.summary: A country where safety is under threat from federal violence on the streets is not fit to stage soccer’s showpiece event Removing the United States as co-host of the 2026 World Cup would hurt for pretty much everyone. Fans would miss out on seeing the sport’s pinnacle in their home towns (or somewhere nearby). Cities and businesses small and large would lose the financial benefits they had banked on. It would be a logistical and political nightmare on an international scale, the likes of which have never been seen before in sports. It would be eminently sad. And it would be entirely justified. It brings me no pleasure to say this. The United States has been eager to host a men’s World Cup for more than a decade and a half. The desire survived and even grew after 2010’s failure to out-bid Russia and Qatar (in public and behind closed doors) for the 2018 and 2022 World Cups. With hosting rights for 2026 later secured alongside Canada and Mexico, the US soccer scene prepared to show off that the sport is now part of the nation’s fabric, 32 years after hosting the tournament for the first time in 1994. Soccer’s growing popularity in America has helped inspire other US sports to try new formats, encouraged us to engage more fully with the world in a sporting context, and has been at the center of conversations about our society and culture. The 2026 World Cup was seen as the best chance for the world to fully experience not just how much the US has improved at soccer, but how much soccer has improved the US. Continue reading...

items.title: Football transfer rumours: Bruno Fernandes to consider Saudi move … again?
items.summary: Today’s rumours are wet It’s been a quiet transfer window, all things considered, with even the worst internet attention-seekers refusing to don their yellow ties and take a day off school for its final day, their mum’s toy spaceship left idling in a shoebox under the bed. But there might yet be some action – not like that, how dare you – so let’s dive in. Crystal Palace are enduring a miserable season, rapidly slipping down the table and now in danger of relegation, the perfect example of how to ruin unexpected success. On the other hand, Steve Parish’s quiff still looks pristine, so swings and roundabouts, but he’s now faced with a problem: does he stop lovingly tending it to consider Nottingham Forest’s £35m bid for Jean-Philippe Mateta, or simply pretend that no such thing ever happened? Continue reading...

items.title: ‘No reason to rush’: Harry Kane in talks with Bayern over contract extension
items.summary: England captain’s deal runs to end of next season Kane and his family are settled in Munich, CEO says Bayern Munich have confirmed they are in talks to extend Harry Kane’s contract. The England captain joined from Tottenham in 2023 on a deal to the end of next season and secured a long-awaited first major trophy when Bayern won the Bundesliga last May. He has been the Bundesliga’s top scorer twice and, with 21 goals in 19 Bundesliga games this season, could chase down Robert Lewandowski’s single-season record of 41 goals. Continue reading...

items.title: Aston Villa in talks to re-sign Luiz
items.summary: Aston Villa are in talks to re-sign midfielder Douglas Luiz 18 months after he left the club for Juventus.
